LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. PLAINTIFFS MICHAEL DIESSNER AND ROBIN DIESSNER'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER OF THE SILVER FUND IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 30, 2008. BOTH PARTIES ARE TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS MICHAEL DIESSNER AND ROBIN DIESSNER'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER OF THE SILVER FUND IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY UNDER CCP SECTION 430.40(B) AND CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1322. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS MICHAEL F. DIESSNER AND ROBIN L. DIESSNER'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT REGARDING SPECIFYING THE ADDITIONAL ALLEGATIONS, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS GRANTED TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF SEEKS ONLY TO ADD PARTIES. SEE RYAN G. V. DEPT. OF TRANSPORTATION (1986) 180 CAL. APP. 3D 1102. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS MICHAEL F. DIESSNER AND ROBIN L. DIESSNER'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - granted. Although plaintiff has not complied with California Rule of Court regarding specifying the additional allegations, plaintiff's motion is granted to the extent plaintiff seeks only to add parties. See Ryan G. v. Dept. of Transportation (1986) 180 Cal. App. 3d 1102. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
